Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and low serum protein/globulin, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy. Cloudy, brown urine with low specific gravity further supports renal involvement. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and assessment for diabetes and renal disease progression.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
